parsed.clarification_question: Should the response focus primarily on technical validity and contribution claims, or on writing and presentation quality?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation invites assessment of the thesis, gap framing, and claimed contribution of ConSenseBench.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating what one thinks of the passage centrally includes checking whether the argument from benchmark design to liveness conclusions is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes performance and liveness claims based on reported benchmark results, so support between evidence and claims is central.
rationale.R1.4: The passage explicitly claims a first standardization contribution, making novelty assessment a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: The artifact relies on prior work by Patel and Ousterhout and makes attribution-heavy claims, so citation and scholarship are central.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must disclose uncertainty because the delegation is very underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid projecting responsibilities beyond what the vague request reasonably supports.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within responsibility assignment rather than evaluating or revising the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading research claims or unsupported evaluation practices downstream.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation wording and artifact features that justify each active responsibility.
